title: angelica turcica
intro: Angelica turcica is a plant species native to the Balkan Peninsula, often referred to as a medicinal or wild herb. It is known for its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve various health issues, including nausea, indigestion, and fever. The plant's medicinal properties include its antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it beneficial for relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and reducing swelling and pain. Angelica turcica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including dried roots, leaves, and flowers.
